sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Terminology 90

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Terminology 90:1
items.html:
(to the question) Pesia leVar [a step outward]
Since the Kli of the Yesod, from its root in AB of AK, is entirely lacking the five Hassadim — although it has of the completeness of Hochma — it is discerned as being outside the spreading of Bina; for Bina "spreads only as far as Hod," and the whole source of the Yesod is only from the spreading of Aba, with a discernment of illumination of Hochma, in the way it had in the Partzuf AB of AK. Therefore, when ZA is in Ibur, the illumination of Ima does not reach to spread to his Kli of Yesod, and it is discerned as being a step outward. However, the Yesod is incorporated in the Tifferet, and from there it receives; but the Malchut, which is incorporated principally in the Yesod, is found to have received his measure, and the Malchut in the Ibur is therefore found in the discernment of a step from outside. (p. 766, Item 50)
